title: Replace wave mode with 3D secondary mode
summary: Add a new volumetric 3D geometry system (CrystalField) as a fourth mode, ensuring no 2D/flat geometries exist. WaveField.ts is noted for deprecation but does not exist in the codebase, so the work is purely additive: create a new sculptural point-based mode, register it in the mode manager, wire quality scaling, and verify all modes are volumetric.

steps.title: Create crystalWarp vertex shader
steps.description: Write a new vertex shader for the crystal/lattice mode. Follow the same uniform interface as pointWarp.vert.glsl (uTime, uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, uMotionAmplitude, uPointerDisturbance, uPointerPos, uPaletteHue, uPaletteSaturation, uCadence, uBreathScale, uBasePointSize, uNoiseFrequency, uRadialScale, uTwistStrength, uFieldSpread, uNoiseOctaves, uEnablePointerRepulsion, uEnableSlowModulation, uDisplacementScale). Use the same attributes (size, aHueOffset, aRandom). Prepend noise3d.glsl at import time. Differentiate from pointCloud by using: (1) crystalline faceted displacement — snapping positions toward lattice planes via quantization + softness blend, (2) bass-driven shattering — points fracture outward along lattice normals when bass spikes, (3) treble-driven facet shimmer — high-frequency angular jitter on lattice edges, (4) slow rotation of the entire lattice basis vectors over time. Include hsl2rgb and the same color pipeline. Keep gl_PointSize clamped identically.
steps.details: The visual identity should feel like a slowly rotating crystalline structure that shatters and reforms with the music. Bass drives macro fracture/expansion, treble drives sparkle on facet edges. The lattice basis can be derived from seed via aRandom offsets.

steps.title: Create crystalWarp fragment shader
steps.description: Create fragment shader for crystal mode. Can reuse the same circular-point-with-softness pattern from pointWarp.frag.glsl (discard outside radius 0.5, smoothstep edge falloff, vColor with uOpacity). Optionally add a subtle angular facet pattern to the alpha falloff to reinforce the crystalline aesthetic (e.g., modulate alpha by an angular step function of gl_PointCoord).
steps.details: Keep it simple — the vertex shader does the heavy lifting. Fragment just needs circular discard, soft edge, opacity uniform, and vColor varying.

steps.title: Add crystal shape generator to volumetricPoints.ts
steps.description: Add a new volumetric shape generator function (e.g., crystalLattice) that produces points arranged in a 3D lattice/crystal structure. Options: (a) BCC or FCC lattice with jitter, (b) Voronoi cell centers in a bounded volume, (c) recursive subdivision of a tetrahedron/octahedron. Add it to the VOLUMETRIC_SHAPES array. The generator should accept count and seed parameters matching the existing signature, return Float32Array of xyz positions filling a unit-scale volume.
steps.details: A simple approach: generate points on a 3D grid (cbrt(count) per axis) within [-1,1]^3, then apply small seeded jitter to each point. This creates a lattice that the shader can then deform. Also consider an icosahedral shell variant for variety.

steps.title: Create CrystalField geometry system
steps.description: Create a new GeometrySystem following the exact pattern of pointCloud.ts. Export CrystalFieldConfig interface (maxPoints, enableSparkle, noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation) and CrystalField interface (extends GeometrySystem with pointCount and positions getters). Export createCrystalField factory function. Implementation: (1) init() — use createPRNG(seed+':crystal') to select shape from volumetricPoints (preferring the new crystal shapes), generate positions, create BufferGeometry with position/size/aHueOffset/aRandom attributes, create ShaderMaterial with crystalWarp shaders (prepend noise3d.glsl), create THREE.Points mesh with AdditiveBlending, add to scene. (2) draw() — update all uniforms from frame.params each frame (same uniform update logic as pointCloud). (3) setOpacity(v) — update uOpacity uniform. (4) cleanup() — remove mesh from scene, dispose geometry and material.
steps.details: Follow pointCloud.ts line-for-line for structure. The only differences are: shader imports (crystalWarp instead of pointWarp), shape selection bias (prefer crystal lattice shapes), and the module name. Reuse the same uniform names so the mapping pipeline works unchanged.

steps.title: Add maxCrystalPoints to QualityProfile
steps.description: Add maxCrystalPoints field to QualityProfile interface. Set tier values: low=200, medium=600, high=1200 (matching pointCloud scaling). This keeps the crystal mode quality-responsive.
steps.details: Add the field to the interface and to each of the three return statements in computeQuality().

steps.title: Register CrystalField in main.ts mode manager
steps.description: Import createCrystalField. Create crystal instance with quality config (maxPoints: quality.maxCrystalPoints, enableSparkle, noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation). Add { name: 'crystal', factory: () => crystal } to the createModeManager array. Add { name: 'crystal', maxPoints: quality.maxCrystalPoints } to the debug modes array.
steps.details: Insert after the pointCloud creation block (~line 111). The mode manager handles all switching logic automatically — no changes needed to modeManager.ts.

steps.title: Verify no 2D/flat geometries remain
steps.description: Search the codebase for any WaveField references, Canvas2D rendering paths, flat plane geometries (THREE.PlaneGeometry, THREE.PlaneBufferGeometry), or 2D line geometries (THREE.Line, THREE.LineSegments used as primary visual). Confirm all four modes (particles, ribbon, pointcloud, crystal) use perspective camera + volumetric point-based geometry. Remove any WaveField.ts if it exists.
steps.details: Acceptance criteria: 'No 2D line or flat plane geometries remain'. Grep for PlaneGeometry, Line, LineSegments, Canvas2D, WaveField, wave references.

steps.title: Build and runtime verification
steps.description: Run npm run build to verify clean compilation. Check for: (1) no TypeScript errors, (2) shader imports resolve correctly via ?raw, (3) no console errors on load, (4) crystal mode renders with visible 3D depth/parallax, (5) bass drives macro deformation, treble drives shimmer, (6) mode switching transitions work with 4 modes, (7) audio pipeline preserved across all mode transitions.
steps.details: Test with ?debug query param to observe mode name cycling through all four modes. Verify the crystal mode name appears in debug overlay.

architectural_notes: All four modes share the same GeometrySystem interface — no changes to modeManager.ts, renderLoop.ts, or the audio pipeline are needed.
The crystal mode reuses the same uniform interface as existing modes, so mappings.ts and evolution.ts work unchanged.
Quality scaling follows the established pattern: QualityProfile drives point counts and feature toggles per tier.
Shader compilation validation (US-048) will automatically cover the new shaders since modeManager.initAllForValidation() iterates all registered modes.
Mode switching with 4 modes still uses round-robin cycling with seeded timing — no logic changes needed.

risks.risk: New shader compile errors breaking the entire scene
risks.mitigation: US-048 shader validation gate catches compile errors before first render. Test shaders incrementally — start with a minimal vertex shader that compiles, then add deformation layers.

risks.risk: 4 modes increasing pre-compilation time on low-end devices
risks.mitigation: cleanupInactive() disposes non-active modes after validation, so runtime cost stays the same. Only startup adds one more shader compile pass.

risks.risk: Crystal lattice points clustering in screen space causing z-fighting or overdraw
risks.mitigation: Use fieldSpread uniform and radialScale to ensure adequate spatial distribution. AdditiveBlending mitigates z-fighting naturally.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.Second mode is volumetric and point-base: Steps 1-4: CrystalField uses THREE.Points + BufferGeometry with 3D lattice positions and perspective camera rendering
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Mode uses 3D deformation: Step 1: crystalWarp vertex shader applies bass-driven fracture, treble shimmer, FBM noise displacement, and twist — all in 3D
acceptance_criteria_mapping.No 2D line or flat plane geometries rema: Step 7: explicit verification sweep for any 2D/flat geometry remnants
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Mode switching preserves audio pipeline: Step 6: CrystalField registers in modeManager which handles transitions; audio pipeline in renderLoop.ts is mode-agnostic
